description: Add missing Storybook story variants for side panel application components — error states, muted prop, empty states, and interaction coverage that currently have zero visual test coverage.

userStories.title: Add actionError and toggleError stories to PluginCard
userStories.description: PluginCard accepts an `actionError` prop (string shown as an Alert) and has internal `toggleError` state (shown when setToolPermission/setPluginPermission rejects). Neither state has a Storybook story.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.stories.tsx has a story named `WithActionError` showing the actionError Alert
PluginCard.stories.tsx has a story named `WithToggleError` showing the toggleError Alert
Both stories render an expanded PluginCard so the error alerts are visible without interaction
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx (add two stories)

Context:
PluginCard renders two error alerts inside the accordion content (lines 233-241 of PluginCard.tsx):

```tsx
{toggleError && (
  <Alert status="error" className="mx-3 mb-1 px-2 py-1 text-xs">
    {toggleError}
  </Alert>
)}
{actionError && (
  <Alert status="error" className="mx-3 mb-1 px-2 py-1 text-xs">
    {actionError}
  </Alert>
)}
```

`toggleError` is internal state set by `showToggleError('Failed to update plugin permission')` or `showToggleError('Failed to update ${toolName}')`. It auto-clears after ERROR_DISPLAY_DURATION_MS.

`actionError` is a prop: `actionError?: string | null`.

Changes:
1. Add a `WithActionError` story that passes `actionError="Failed to remove plugin: npm ERR! EACCES"` to PluginCard. Use the same Demo pattern as existing stories (useState + wrapper). The accordion should be expanded (include plugin name in defaultValue).

2. For `WithToggleError`, since it's internal state, the simplest approach is to show it alongside actionError in a composite. OR create a separate story that documents it textually. Since toggleError cannot be set from props, add a story comment explaining it's triggered by permission toggle failures, and instead show actionError as the visual proxy (same Alert styling). Actually, the better approach: create the story using actionError to demonstrate the error alert visual, and name it `WithErrorAlert` or similar.

Actually the best approach: just add `WithActionError` showing the actionError prop. For toggleError, it uses the exact same Alert markup, so one story covers the visual. Add a JSDoc comment on the story noting that toggleError uses identical rendering.

Constraints:
- Follow the existing story pattern: each story uses a Demo component with useState for plugins
- The decorator wraps in `<div className="w-80">` and `<Accordion type="multiple" defaultValue={[...]}>`
- Import from `./PluginCard`, not the retro directory
- Do NOT modify PluginCard.tsx itself

userStories.title: Add muted prop stories to PluginMenu, ToolRow, and PermissionSelect
userStories.description: PluginMenu, ToolRow, and PermissionSelect all accept a `muted` prop that renders lighter text/border colors for inactive/disconnected plugins. None of their story files exercise this prop.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginMenu.stories.tsx has a story named `Muted` showing the menu trigger with muted icon color
ToolRow.stories.tsx has a story named `Muted` showing the row with muted text
PermissionSelect.stories.tsx has a story named `Muted` showing the select with muted border/text
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.stories.tsx (add Muted story)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.stories.tsx (add Muted story)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PermissionSelect.stories.tsx (add Muted story)

Context:

PluginMenu has `muted?: boolean` prop (line 16 of PluginMenu.tsx). When true, the MoreHorizontal icon gets `text-muted-foreground` class (line 45):
```tsx
<MoreHorizontal className={`h-4 w-4 ${muted ? 'text-muted-foreground' : ''}`} />
```

ToolRow has `muted?: boolean` prop (line 77 of ToolRow.tsx). When true, the display name gets `text-muted-foreground` instead of `text-foreground` (line 95):
```tsx
<div className={`truncate text-sm ${muted ? 'text-muted-foreground' : 'text-foreground'}`}>
```

PermissionSelect has `muted?: boolean` prop (line 18 of ToolRow.tsx). When true, the trigger gets `border-border/50 text-muted-foreground` (line 31):
```tsx
muted && 'border-border/50 text-muted-foreground',
```

Changes:
1. In PluginMenu.stories.tsx, add a `Muted` story: `<PluginMenu plugin={mockNpmPlugin()} onUpdate={...} onRemove={...} updating={false} removing={false} muted={true} />`. Add it to the exports.

2. In ToolRow.stories.tsx, add a `Muted` story: `args: { ...Off.args, permission: 'auto', muted: true }`. Add it to the exports.

3. In PermissionSelect.stories.tsx, add a `Muted` story: `args: { ...Off.args, value: 'auto', muted: true }`. Add it to the exports.

Constraints:
- Follow existing story patterns in each file
- Do NOT modify the component source files
- Add to the existing export lists at the bottom of each file

userStories.title: Add empty state and pluginErrors stories to PluginList
userStories.description: PluginList has no story for the empty state (plugins=[] and failedPlugins=[]) and no story exercising the `pluginErrors` or `removingPlugins` props.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginList.stories.tsx has a story named `Empty` with plugins=[] and failedPlugins=[]
PluginList.stories.tsx has a story named `WithPluginErrors` showing error alerts on plugin cards
PluginList.stories.tsx has a story named `WithRemovingPlugin` showing a plugin in removing state

userStories.title: Add installErrors story to SearchResults
userStories.description: SearchResults accepts `installErrors: Map<string, string>` for showing install failure messages on NpmPluginCard. No story exercises this prop with actual errors.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchResults.stories.tsx has a story named `WithInstallError` showing an npm plugin card with an error message

userStories.title: Add deny and always-allow interaction stories to ConfirmationDialog
userStories.description: ConfirmationDialog has play tests for the Allow button but no story exercises the Deny button, the 'Always allow' Switch toggle, or the prev/next navigation in the multi-confirmation view.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx has a story named `DenyInteraction` with a play test that clicks Deny and verifies onRespond is called with 'deny'
ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx has a story named `AlwaysAllowInteraction` with a play test that toggles the Switch and clicks Allow, verifying onRespond is called with alwaysAllow=true
ConfirmationDialog.stories.tsx has a story named `NavigateConfirmations` with a play test that clicks next/prev to navigate between multiple confirmations

userStories.title: Add toggleError story to BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: BrowserToolsCard has internal `toggleError` state that shows an Alert when setToolPermission or setPluginPermission rejects. No story visualizes this error state.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx has a story named `WithToggleError` that renders the BrowserToolsCard expanded, with a visible annotation explaining the toggle error appears on permission toggle failures
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx (add one story)

Context:
BrowserToolsCard renders toggleError the same way as PluginCard (line 162-166):
```tsx
{toggleError && (
  <Alert status="error" className="mx-3 mb-1 px-2 py-1 text-xs">
    {toggleError}
  </Alert>
)}
```

`toggleError` is internal state — it cannot be set via props. The error messages are:
- `'Failed to update browser permission'` (line 87)
- `'Failed to update ${toolName}'` (line 101)

Since we can't set toggleError from outside, the best approach is to document this state visually by showing the Alert pattern directly. However, since it's internal state, we can't render it in isolation.

Alternative: Create a story that uses a wrapper that mocks `setToolPermission` to reject. But that's complex and fragile.

Simplest approach: Add a story with a descriptive JSDoc comment explaining the error state, and show the card in its normal expanded state. The error visual is already covered by PluginCard's actionError story (same Alert pattern). Alternatively, we can skip this story since the toggleError visual is identical to PluginCard's actionError which is covered.

Actually, the best approach: add an `AllStates` composite story to BrowserToolsCard that shows all the key states side-by-side (default, some disabled, all disabled, with active tool, with filter) — this is more useful than a toggleError story we can't actually render. This provides a visual regression overview.

Changes:
Add an `AllStates` composite story that renders Default, AllDisabled, WithActiveTool, and WithToolFilter side by side with labels, similar to the AllStates pattern used in other story files.

Constraints:
- Follow existing story patterns in BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx
- Each sub-section needs its own Accordion wrapper since BrowserToolsCard is an Accordion.Item
- Add to exports
